取引先や外部パートナーと資料、画像、見積書などを一時的に受け渡すためのグループル: Explains how to use a group room for temporary file exchanges with clients and external partners, from setup through cleanup.
FS!QR Groupを使えば、ブラウザからグループルームを作り、取引先や外部パ: With FS!QR Group you can create a group room from your browser and exchange files with clients and outside partners on a temporary basis. Give each project its own sharing space and keep file names and deadlines consistent, and external hand-offs become far easier to manage.
「最新版.pdf」「資料2.pdf」のような名前は、やり取りが増えるほど混乱の原: Names like "latest.pdf" or "document2.pdf" breed confusion as the exchange grows. The more external the audience, the more the file name itself should tell the story.
いつまで利用するか: How long it will be used
おすすめは、日付、案件名、用途、版を入れる形式です。: Our recommendation is a format that includes the date, the project, the purpose, and the version.
こうした場面では、案件や期間ごとに一時的なグループルームを作り、必要なファイルだ: Situations like these call for a temporary group room per project or per period, holding only the files that matter. With a single agreed place to share, both senders and receivers always know exactly where to look.
この5点を決めてからルームを案内すれば、相手が迷いにくくなります。ルーム名にも案: Settle these five points before sending the invitation and the other side won't get lost. Put the project name or date in the room name too — it makes rooms easy to tell apart when several exchanges run in parallel.
すべてのファイルを同じ扱いにすると、便利さは増えますがリスクも増えます。公開資料: Treat every file the same way and the convenience grows — but so does the risk. Public materials, review images, quotes, contracts, and files containing personal data each warrant a different level of care.
たとえば、次のような文面にすると実務で使いやすいです。: For example, wording like this works well in practice.
たとえば「確認後、このメールに受領済みと返信してください」「差し替えが必要な場合: Spell out the response you expect: "once reviewed, please reply 'received' to this email", or "if a revision is needed, let us know by 5 p.m. today". A shared room is a place to put files — it doesn't manage confirmations or sign-offs for you. The sentence that prompts a human to act is what completes the loop.
どの形式のファイルを置くか: Which file formats will be placed there
グループルームは手軽に作れますが、先に用途を決めておくと運用が安定します。特に外: Group rooms are quick to create, but deciding the purpose first keeps the operation steady. With external participants especially, keep the explanation shorter and the rules clearer than you would internally.
ファイルをアップロードしただけでは、相手が確認したかどうか分かりません。取引先と: Uploading a file tells you nothing about whether the other side saw it. In client work, build a one-line receipt confirmation into the routine alongside the file sharing.
フォルダ代わりにファイル名のルールをそろえる: Standardise file names instead of building folders
メール添付と共有ルームを使い分ける: Use email attachments and shared rooms for what each does best
メール添付は、少数の相手へ小さなファイルを送るだけなら便利です。一方で、複数人が: Email attachments are fine for sending small files to a few people. For projects with several participants, documents that get replaced, or large files, a shared room is far easier to manage.
ルームを作る前に決めておくこと: Decide before you create the room
一時共有では、複雑なフォルダ構成を作るよりも、ファイル名のルールをそろえるほうが: In temporary sharing, agreeing on file-name rules often beats building an elaborate folder tree. Even after the other side downloads everything, the names alone still tell them what each file is.
一時共有は、終わらせるところまで含めて運用です。共有開始時に終了日を決めておくと: Temporary sharing isn't finished until it's wound down. Set the end date when the sharing starts and the clean-up won't be forgotten.
不要な中間ファイルや古い版を削除する: Delete interim files and superseded versions
何の案件、会議、納品のためのルームか: Which deal, meeting, or delivery the room is for
公開しても問題ない資料はグループルームでまとめて配布し、機密度の高い資料はパスワ: Distribute freely shareable materials together in the group room, and give highly confidential ones tighter handling — password protection and shorter expiry. When sending access details, don't put the URL and the password in the same message; use separate routes and the risk drops.

取引先とのファイル共有は「一時的な場所」を作ると管理しやすい: A "temporary place" makes client file exchanges manageable
取引先との一時共有で最も避けたいのは、案件が終わった後も古いファイルや共有情報が: The thing to avoid most in temporary client sharing is old files and access details outliving the deal. Materials from unrelated projects start mixing together, and people who no longer need access still have it — a standing management risk.
取引先に案内するときの文面: Wording the invitation to a client
取引先へ共有終了を連絡する: Tell the client the sharing has ended
取引先や外部パートナーとファイルをやり取りするとき、メール添付だけで進めると、最: Exchanging files with clients and outside partners by email attachment alone soon goes wrong: nobody knows which version is current, size limits block sending, and it's hard to trace who received what. Pasting files into chat works too — until the project drags on and yesterday's attachments sink out of sight.
受け取り確認を運用に入れる: Build receipt confirmation into the routine
外部の相手に共有ルームを案内するときは、URLだけを送らず、目的と操作を一緒に伝: When inviting an outside party into a shared room, never send a bare URL — explain the purpose and the steps together. Mention that no account or special app is needed, and even a first-time user comes in without friction.
必要なファイルを社内の正式な保管場所へ移す: Move the files worth keeping into your company's official storage

The quote and the review images are already uploaded; please take a look. If you have additional documents, please upload them to the same room.
案内文には、目的、置いてあるファイル、相手にしてほしい行動、利用期限を含めます。: The invitation should cover the purpose, the files placed there, the action you'd like taken, and the usage deadline. That alone cuts down the back-and-forth questions from the other side.
機密度に応じて共有方法を変える: Match the sharing method to the sensitivity
次回用に案内文やファイル名ルールだけ残す: Keep only the invitation wording and the file-name rules for next time
終了後：ルームを残しっぱなしにしない: Afterwards: don't leave the room sitting there
